 {"id":"ft-7quzz","title":"headless_mux_server: server_config_serde test missing max_peers after ft-ry224 added the field","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/headless_mux_server.rs:1116 has a ServerConfig struct literal in test server_config_serde that pre-dates ft-ry224's addition of max_peers: u32 (line 120). The test now fails to compile with E0063. Trivial: add max_peers: <value> to the literal.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:43:29.638577Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:51:45.829569Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:51:45.829266Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-7s0vw","title":"event_id::detect_clock_anomaly overflows on prev + future_skew_threshold","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_id.rs:205\` computes
\`prev + future_skew_threshold\` without overflow protection. In release
builds u64 wraps silently; in debug builds the unchecked add panics.
Either way, a pathological or adversarial \`prev\` value breaks the
future-skew branch.

## Concrete failure path

1. Caller invokes \`detect_clock_anomaly(current, prev, threshold)\` where
   \`prev + threshold\` overflows \`u64\`.
2. Easy reach: replay/import flows feeding \`recorded_at_ms = u64::MAX\` from
   a corrupt fixture, fuzzer, or attacker-controlled input. Also reachable
   via legitimate-but-distant clock sources (UNIX epoch micros around year
   586,524 if anyone reaches that, or any monotonic clock that has been
   bumped by a process bug into the high u64 range).
3. **Release build:** \`prev + threshold\` wraps to a tiny value. The
   condition \`current > tiny_value\` is now true for almost any \`current\`,
   so the function returns \`is_anomaly: true\` for every subsequent valid
   timestamp — flooding telemetry with false-positive "future skew"
   reports and potentially triggering downstream remediation that should
   not have fired.
4. **Debug build:** unchecked add panics (\`attempt to add with overflow\`),
   killing whatever thread was processing the recorder pipeline.
5. \`ClockAnomalyTracker::observe\` always updates the baseline
   ("Updates the baseline regardless") — so a single malicious or
   transiently-high \`current\` value sticks around and continues to
   poison overflow checks against subsequent observations.

## Severity rationale

- Silent wrong answer in release; thread-killing panic in debug.
- Adversary-reachable via fixture/replay/fuzz inputs.
- Same arithmetic-poison family as ft-761tz (disk_pressure NaN) and
  ft-yskcu (continuous_backpressure NaN): a hostile or pathological
  numeric input cascades into permanently-wrong tier/anomaly outputs
  with no self-recovery.

## Fix sketch

1. Replace \`prev + future_skew_threshold\` with \`prev.checked_add(threshold)\`.
2. On overflow, treat as "no future-skew anomaly" — we cannot prove a
   timestamp is too far in the future relative to a near-MAX baseline,
   so the only sound default is \"don't flag\". Same disposition as
   ft-761tz: when the math itself becomes unreliable, fail toward
   silence rather than toward false positives.
3. Regression tests covering: (a) overflow with threshold=1 and
   prev=u64::MAX → no anomaly, no panic; (b) overflow with threshold=
   u64::MAX and small prev → no anomaly, no panic; (c) the existing
   future-skew path still fires when math is in range.

## Scope

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_id.rs\` only. No cross-crate API
changes. \`ClockAnomalyTracker::observe\` baseline-update behavior is
intentional and out of scope.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:30:32.110899Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:36:43.255991Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:36:43.255609Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 4431483f: detect_clock_anomaly now uses prev.checked_add(future_skew_threshold), failing silent on overflow rather than panicking (debug) or wrapping (release). 5 regression tests added covering (current=prev=u64::MAX, threshold=1), (huge threshold), (boundary u64::MAX × u64::MAX), (in-range path still fires), and (tracker survives MAX baseline). All 53 event_id tests pass against /tmp/ft-rv-p0-target. Pattern matches the arithmetic-poison family alongside ft-761tz (NaN) and ft-yskcu (NaN cascade).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-7tq4z","title":"[HIGH] SCHEMA_SQL drift: fresh DBs skip v24 policy_denied_audit table (storage.rs:4440-4446 sets user_version=SCHEMA_VERSION without running migrations)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:52:09.392483Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T02:16:44.933511Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T02:16:44.933247Z","close_reason":"92cd7452 fixes the SCHEMA_SQL drift by collapsing the two current==0 branches in initialize_schema into a single path through run_v0_init_in_transaction. The truly-fresh DB case now goes through repair (no-op when no tables exist) → SCHEMA_SQL → run_migrations(0) → ensure_ft_meta — same atomicity contract as ft-k542h/c06b230a. The buggy early-return that ran SCHEMA_SQL and stamped user_version=SCHEMA_VERSION directly (skipping run_migrations entirely) is deleted; needs_init is preserved for other callers (status reporters/diagnostics). Three test changes: (1) NEW fresh_db_init_creates_policy_denied_audit_via_v24_migration pins the regression — fresh DB has user_version==SCHEMA_VERSION AND the v24 policy_denied_audit table with all documented columns AND a record_policy_denial_audit_sync insert succeeds; (2) UPDATED schema_version_audit_trail_recorded asserts the new invariant (count>=1, max(version)==SCHEMA_VERSION, every version in 1..=SCHEMA_VERSION with non-null applied_at) instead of the old hardcoded count==1 / 'Initial schema'; (3) UPDATED idempotent_init_preserves_version compares snapshots (count_after_first==count_after_second) instead of hardcoding 1 — robust against MIGRATIONS growing. CLEANUP: ft-cro2u's wait_for_tool_persists test no longer needs the v24 CREATE TABLE workaround. 188 storage::tests pass via rch on /tmp/ft-cc6-target (3 unrelated recorder_storage frankensqlite gate failures pre-exist this commit).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","schema","storage"],"comments":[{"id":3641,"issue_id":"ft-7tq4z","author":"jemanuel","text":"# Discovered while writing ft-cro2u

Pre-existing bug surfaced by the new audit-persist test in ft-cro2u.
The fresh-DB initialization path at
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:4440-4446` is:

    if current == 0 && needs_init {
        conn.execute_batch(SCHEMA_SQL)?;
        set_user_version(conn, SCHEMA_VERSION)?;
        record_migration(conn, SCHEMA_VERSION, "Initial schema")?;
        ensure_ft_meta(conn, SCHEMA_VERSION)?;
        return Ok(());
    }

It runs the static `SCHEMA_SQL` blob and stamps the user_version
directly to `SCHEMA_VERSION` (currently 24) — but `SCHEMA_SQL` does
NOT include the `policy_denied_audit` table that landed in migration
v24. New databases never get that table created.

# Repro

A brand-new SQLite file opened via `StorageHandle::new(path)` has
`user_version = 24` (per `SCHEMA_VERSION`) but
`SELECT * FROM policy_denied_audit` returns
`SqliteFailure: no such table: policy_denied_audit`.

# Impact

* Every MCP policy-deny on a fresh DB silently fails the audit-persist
  step (`persist_mcp_policy_denial_async` warn-logs and continues).
  Operators get a denied response in tracing but no row in
  `policy_denied_audit` to query later.
* This affects new installs / new test harnesses / fresh CI workspaces.
  Existing user DBs that walked through migrations (v0 → v1 → … → v24)
  DID get the table because v24's `up_sql` ran for them.

# Two fixes

1. **Append CREATE TABLE policy_denied_audit (and its two indexes) to
   SCHEMA_SQL.** Each `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` is idempotent so
   existing migrated DBs won't double-create. Cheap. Makes fresh DBs
   match the migrated-up DBs.
2. **Run migrations on the fresh-DB path.** Replace the early-return
   with `conn.execute_batch(SCHEMA_SQL)?; set_user_version(conn, 0)?;
   run_migrations(conn, 0)?;` so every migration helper applies. More
   thorough but also runs every helper for tables SCHEMA_SQL already
   created — harmless idempotently, but extra work.

(1) is the smaller change and matches the documented invariant that
SCHEMA_SQL == "the canonical schema at the latest version". Fix (2)
would be the more general structural answer.

# Workaround in test scope (ft-cro2u)

The new `wait_for_tool_persists_policy_denial_audit_when_storage_is_attached`
test pre-seeds the table with a direct rusqlite connection before
invoking the tool, with an inline comment pointing at this bead. When
this bead lands the workaround block can be deleted.

# Out of scope

Other v24+ migrations should also be audited for SCHEMA_SQL drift —
this might not be the only orphan.","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:52:32Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-7v53r","title":"[codebase-audit][mux/term/codec] mux pool socket-disappearance fault injection: no test exercises mid-RPC socket loss","description":"## Domain gap

The `frankenterm/mux` vendored crate plus `crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs`
maintain a connection pool to the live wezterm mux socket
(`~/.local/share/wezterm/default.sock` per the README's vendored
config). Existing benches:

- `benches/mux_client_ops.rs` — happy-path RPC throughput
- `benches/mux_pool_scaling.rs` — pool size under load
- `tests/conformance_pdu_wire.rs` — codec frame correctness

What's missing: **fault injection where the underlying socket
disappears mid-operation**. No test exercises:

- Socket file deleted while a connection is held.
- mux server killed (SIGKILL) mid-RPC.
- Socket file replaced (atomic rename) mid-RPC.
- File-system unmounted (e.g., USB_NVME volume disappearing per
  the AGENTS.md note).

## Why this is a real defect

The README markets ft as a swarm-native control plane. Operators
hot-swap mux servers all the time — wezterm itself crashes,
Docker volumes detach, the socket gets `rm`'d during cleanup.
Every one of these is a "socket disappears mid-RPC" event.

Concrete failure modes that no current test catches:

1. **Stuck pool entries.** If the connection pool holds a
   broken connection and a caller `acquire()`s it, the next RPC
   blocks until OS-level timeout (TCP-style hang on a Unix
   socket — minutes, not seconds).

2. **Pool exhaustion via stuck entries.** If broken connections
   accumulate without eviction, the pool's `max_connections`
   quota fills with dead entries. New callers wait forever.

3. **Cancellation propagation.** A Cx canceled while waiting on
   a broken socket — does asupersync's cancellation actually
   wake the syscall? Per MEMORY.md's
   `asupersync-midflight-cancel-gap`, the answer is "not always."

The skill's domain-audit lens specifically catches this: every
prior audit (fuzzing, profiling, security, conformance, etc.)
focused on the input data flowing through the mux. None
exercised the mux as a faulted dependency.

## Suggested approach

1. **Fault-injection test harness** at
   `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_socket_fault_injection.rs`:
   - Spawn a stub mux server bound to a temp Unix socket.
   - Start a steady-state RPC loop from the production
     `WeztermClient` (real code path, not mock).
   - Under `tokio::time::pause` (or asupersync equivalent),
     inject:
       a. Server `kill -9` mid-RPC.
       b. `unlink(socket_path)` while pool holds connections.
       c. `rename(socket_path, new_path)` (atomic swap).
   - Assert: every in-flight RPC returns `Err` with a
     specific error variant (not hang, not panic).
   - Assert: subsequent `acquire()` calls succeed against a
     restarted server within N seconds (pool self-heals).

2. **Add a `mux_pool.health_check_ms` config knob** for periodic
   liveness pings; broken connections evicted before the next
   acquire.

3. **Cancellation-correctness test**: spawn a long blocking
   RPC, cancel the Cx, assert the await wakes within a bounded
   deadline.

4. **Telemetry**: counter for `mux.socket_lost_events`,
   `mux.connection_evictions`, `mux.acquire_wait_p95`. Surface
   in `ft doctor`.

## Out of scope

- Implementing automatic mux-server restart (operator concern).
- Cross-host transport (distributed mode is its own surface;
  filed earlier as ft-h8v8v fuzz target for WireEnvelope).

## Acceptance criteria

- `tests/mux_socket_fault_injection.rs` exists with at least
  three injection scenarios.
- Each test asserts bounded recovery time (< 5s) and no
  panic/hang.
- Cancellation-correctness test covers the
  asupersync-midflight-cancel path.
- Telemetry counters land + appear in `ft doctor` output.

## References

- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs` (pool definition,
  mux RPC entry point)
- `frankenterm/mux/` (vendored mux crate)
- README §Vendored config (`mux_socket_path`)
- MEMORY.md `asupersync-midflight-cancel-gap`
- Skill: codebase-audit — domain coverage matrix

Filed by cc_1 codebase audit, 2026-04-28.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T10:27:44.808867Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T20:48:31.257230Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-7ycug","title":"[review][LOW] ea7f89f9 GUI test coverage: tests assert type stability, not rendering correctness","description":"ft-1memj.28 commit ea7f89f9 adds 5 GUI unit tests across crates/frankenterm-gui/src/{glyphcache,quad,overlay/quickselect}.rs. Audit finding: tests pass superficial coverage bar but leave real rendering edges uncovered.

Specifics:

1. glyphcache.rs test_atlas_rect_lookup and atlas_rect_lookup_separates_distinct_cached_colors verify that cached_color memoizes and that distinct colors get distinct origins. That's a hash-map property test, not an atlas-rect-lookup test. Zero coverage for:
   - Atlas-full path (OutOfTextureSpace branch in glyphcache).
   - 2x HiDPI scale — cached_color takes only (color, alpha), no DPI param; glyph rasterization at 2x scale is untested.
   - Unicode wide-width / emoji / combining marks — glyphcache has separate code paths for these (see Underline + combining handling in the same file).
   - Atlas eviction under pressure.

2. overlay/quickselect.rs extracts two helpers (merge_dirty_results, clear_rendered_dirty_result) and tests them. clear_rendered_dirty_result is a one-line wrapper around RangeSet::remove with zero added logic — the test is effectively 'does RangeSet::remove work'. merge_dirty_results test is legitimate coverage of the intersect+clip behavior. But neither exercises:
   - sequence-number ordering of dirty merges during active search.
   - dirty-row propagation when search_row changes (the line 611/705 call sites interact with pattern-match highlighting).

3. quad.rs test_cell_vertex_generation overlaps with the existing quad_propagates_colors_* test two asserts below it. Both call set_position + set_texture_discrete and check vertex coordinates. Incremental coverage value is near zero.

Verdict: coverage-metric churn, not regression-catching tests. The ft-1memj.28 bead was 'add gui unit test coverage' so the commit technically closes it, but the tests would not have caught historical GUI bugs (atlas overflow, HiDPI scaling, wide-glyph clipping, dirty-row propagation during search).

Severity: LOW — not a bug, but a test-quality finding to flag for future test passes. Not recommending a fix commit here; file for visibility when the next ft-1memj.x GUI test pass lands.

Tag: review. Discovered in reviewer pane audit round 2, 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:02:10.940194Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:15:23.709618Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:15:23.709104Z","close_reason":"Landed commit a8bf4853 with stronger GUI unit assertions: exact row/col vertex positions, exact first-slot atlas UV tuple for a known block glyph, atlas-pressure growth/no-eviction behavior, and precise quick-select dirty-row merge/search-row propagation checks. Remote verification ran twice via rch and reached real compilation, but both Linux workers failed before test execution because the required CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang path does not exist there during openssl-sys setup.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-812ei","title":"[audit] mock-code-finder sweep saturated for core/{robot_types,events,redactor,workflows/handlers}","description":"Round-3 mock-code-finder sweep across 4 production files in crates/frankenterm-core/src/:

- robot_types.rs (5736 lines / ~200 KB) — robot CLI wire/data types
- events.rs (1589 production lines / ~147 KB) — async event bus + lifecycle events
- redactor.rs (~7.7 KB) — sensitive-data redaction filter (15 regex patterns: OpenAI, Anthropic, GitHub, AWS, Bearer, Slack, Stripe, DB URLs, OAuth, etc.)
- workflows/handlers.rs (8786 lines) — workflow execution handlers

Categories audited (production code only, #[cfg(test)] excluded):
1. unimplemented!()/todo!()/unreachable!()
2. Ok(())/Default::default()/None returns with no real work
3. Hard-coded sentinel return values
4. FIXME/XXX/STUB/FAKE/PLACEHOLDER/TODO/HACK comments
5. mock_*/fake_*/stub_*/dummy_* outside #[cfg(test)]
6. panic!("not implemented") variants
7. Err(...) always-returned with "not yet implemented"
8. Async-specific: spawn-and-forget tasks; unbounded-send hot paths
9. Redactor-specific: pass-through input (silent leak)
10. Workflows-specific: handlers returning Ok(Success) without side effects

Result: zero production stubs across all 4 files. The redactor's 15 secret patterns are all substantive matchers (no pass-through). events.rs uses bounded broadcast channels with explicit backpressure handling and proper `select!`-based cancellation. workflows/handlers.rs sampled execute_step impls (lines 389, 1080, 2549) all perform substantive async work. robot_types.rs is data-only with no incomplete trait impls.

No bead filed.

Cross-references:
- ft-n3c2q (round-2 sweep — replay/ars/tantivy sub-crates)
- Earlier: project_mux_term_codec_sweep.md, project_capture_storage_sweep_findings.md, project_patterns_sweep_findings.md","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:12:25.139581Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T19:12:25.139581Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T19:12:25.139581Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Next step is to rerun the rch-backed vendored_async_contract_behavioral lane against the current tree and verify that the earlier frankenterm-term compile blocker is cleared.","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:48:44Z"},{"id":2565,"issue_id":"ft-p48pw.8","author":"jemanuel","text":"Validation unblock complete: reran the targeted rch-backed vendored_async_contract_behavioral lane against the current tree and the earlier frankenterm-term compile blocker did not recur. Command used env TMPDIR=/tmp CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-ft-p48pw7b cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test vendored_async_contract_behavioral --features vendored,asupersync-runtime b23 -- --nocapture on worker vmi1149989; result PASS with 6 tests passed / 0 failed.","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:58:19Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-p48pw.9","title":"[ft-p48pw][support] Add external explicit-Cx get-lines/write-to-pane cancellation coverage","description":"Support slice for ft-p48pw: add external behavioral coverage proving get_lines_with_cx and write_to_pane_with_cx fail fast for a pre-cancelled Cx and emit no post-handshake request frames. -{"id":"ft-pe4ds","title":"[codebase-audit][robot/MCP] error-envelope schema is unvalidated — runtime gate (ft-5ikbd) only covers success_envelope","description":"## Domain gap

ft-5ikbd (commit 04911fff7) shipped the runtime jsonschema validator
for robot envelopes. The fixture loader at
`tests/conformance_robot_envelope_schema.rs:116` extracts the
**`success_envelope`** field from each `wa_*.json` golden.

The MCP/robot envelope schema (`docs/json-schema/wa-robot-envelope.json`)
covers BOTH success and failure shapes via the `if/then` clause:

```json
"if": { "properties": { "ok": { "const": true } } },
"then": { "required": ["data"] }
```

…and the schema separately requires `error` + `error_code`
fields with a `^robot\\.[a-z_]+$` pattern when `ok=false`.

**The runtime validator never exercises the `ok=false` branch
against real fixtures.** The `wa_*.json` fixtures only carry a
`success_envelope` field. Error envelopes ARE produced in
production (`MCP_ERR_PANE_NOT_FOUND`, `MCP_ERR_POLICY`,
`MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS` per `mcp_tools.rs`), but no golden pins
their shape and no test validates them.

## Why this is a real defect

Concrete failure scenarios:

1. **A new error variant ships with a malformed `error_code`.**
   The schema requires `^robot\\.[a-z_]+$`. A code like
   `robot.not-implemented` (hyphen) or `robot.NotFound` (caps)
   would slip through today because no test runs an error
   envelope through the validator.

2. **A `data: null` leak.** Per the schema's `if/then`,
   when `ok=true`, `data` MUST be present. The complement —
   when `ok=false`, the `data` field is OPTIONAL but if
   present it must be parseable. A handler that returns
   `ok=false` PLUS a malformed `data` slips by.

3. **Hint string contains a secret.** The `hint` field is
   free-form. ft-3se13 (security audit) catches this for
   the decision log; the same risk exists at the response
   surface but no current test asserts the hint goes through
   redaction.

ft-5ikbd's three falsification gates
(`missing-required-field`, `malformed-error-code`,
`ok-true-without-data`) prove the validator works on
SYNTHETIC error envelopes — but no REAL production error
path is tested.

## Suggested approach

1. **Extend the fixture corpus** at
   `tests/golden_robot_envelope/`:
   - Add a sibling `error_envelope` field to each `wa_*.json`
     fixture that has a known error path (e.g., `wa_state.json`
     with the "wezterm not running" error).
   - Or: add new `wa_*_error.json` fixtures that pin specific
     error variants (one per error_code constant in
     `mcp_tools.rs`).

2. **Extend the runtime validator** at
   `tests/conformance_robot_envelope_schema.rs`:
   - Add a parallel `every_golden_error_envelope_validates_against_schema`
     test that loads `error_envelope` (or the new error fixtures)
     and validates each against the schema.
   - Add a coverage assertion: every error_code constant in
     production code (`mcp_tools.rs::MCP_ERR_*`) MUST appear
     in at least one fixture (so adding a new error_code
     forces a fixture update).

3. **Production hint redaction**: route every `McpEnvelope::error`
   call through `engine.redact_secrets()` for the `hint` field
   before it leaves the process. Mirror ft-3se13's audit-log fix.

## Out of scope

- The synthetic falsification gates already in ft-5ikbd (they're
  fine; this bead extends real-fixture coverage).
- The MCP coverage matrix (ft-zaqi8 — that's a higher-level
  spec/test-mapping work).

## Acceptance criteria

- ≥10 error envelope fixtures pinned (one per error_code constant
  used in production).
- A new test validates each against the schema.
- The coverage assertion fails red when a new `MCP_ERR_*` constant
  is added without a fixture.
- Production `McpEnvelope::error` redacts the hint field.

## References

- `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/conformance_robot_envelope_schema.rs:116`
  (the success-only fixture loader)
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs` (`MCP_ERR_*`
  constants)
- `docs/json-schema/wa-robot-envelope.json` (schema covers both
  branches)
- ft-5ikbd (sibling: runtime success-envelope validator —
  already shipped)
- ft-3se13 (sibling: secrets in audit log — same
  redactor-on-write principle applies to hint field)

Filed by cc_1 codebase audit, 2026-04-28.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod_4","created_at":"2026-04-28T10:28:16.531725Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T20:38:46.935743Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-phbu5","title":"[HIGH] full_pipeline_mux_checkpoint_topology still cannot verify pane-tree rollback","description":"Commit 30172171 fixes the vacuous baseline roundtrip, but the test still reduces "topology" to a pane-id set derived from LifecycleRegistry records rather than the restored topology payload.

Evidence:
- `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/integration_mux_checkpoint_topology.rs:392-423` now compares only `server.registry().snapshot()` pane IDs/count against the baseline and then re-parses `initial_topo_json` as a secondary serde check.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_topology.rs:38-89` defines topology as windows, tabs, `active_tab_index`, and recursive `pane_tree`, not just pane membership.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/durable_state.rs:159-167,288-314` shows HeadlessMuxServer checkpoints/rollback persist and rebuild only `LifecycleRegistry::snapshot()` entities; there is no topology JSON in the checkpoint path for the test to compare after rollback.

Why this matters:
- A rollback that preserves the same pane IDs but restores the wrong tab grouping, active tab, or split tree would still satisfy the current assertions.
- The test name and comments still claim topology restoration coverage, so this is false confidence on the checkpoint/rollback surface.

Expected fix:
- Either persist/restore topology payloads in the checkpoint path and compare a post-rollback `TopologySnapshot` against the baseline,
- or explicitly narrow/rename this test to registry-membership rollback and add a separate topology persistence/restore integration test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T06:58:05.819749Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:23:00.021289Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:23:00.020891Z","close_reason":"Strengthened full_pipeline_mux_checkpoint_topology with structural pane-tree rollback invariant","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-phz7x","title":"codec: buffered_frame_len accepts oversized tagged_len, allowing memory-exhaustion before MAX_PDU_SIZE fires","description":"frankenterm/codec/src/lib.rs:240 buffered_frame_len validates only that the leb128-decoded payload_len fits in usize. It does NOT check against MAX_PDU_SIZE (256 MiB at lib.rs:106). An attacker who sends a frame whose outer tagged_len advertises e.g. 10 GiB forces try_read_and_decode at lib.rs:763-792 into a read loop that keeps extending the caller's buffer 4 KiB at a time until buffer.len() >= total_len — because stream_decode's first step is if buffered_frame_len(buffer)?.is_none() { return Ok(None); }, which short-circuits to 'need more bytes' and never rejects oversize. decode_raw at lib.rs:395-403 DOES reject via MAX_PDU_SIZE, but by then the process has already OOM'd or swapped. Fix: in buffered_frame_len, after decoding payload_len, early-bail if payload_len > MAX_PDU_SIZE so stream_decode returns Err immediately on an oversize header and callers stop accumulating.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:22:57.249807Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:27:53.618256Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:27:53.617831Z","close_reason":"Landed in 22965865 (fix(codec): reject oversize tagged_len before accumulation in buffered_frame_len). Concurrent-agent commit landed the identical fix + regression test I wrote: MAX_PDU_SIZE early-reject in buffered_frame_len before try_read_and_decode accumulates attacker-advertised gigabytes. Test buffered_frame_len_rejects_oversize_tagged_len_ft_phz7x passes in the 117-test codec suite.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-pmpxj","title":"vendored mux_client pane-delta helper regression blocks frankenterm-core build","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:59:50.368556Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:09:43.522568Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:09:43.522022Z","close_reason":"Verified in bd9b2d99; narrow vendored rebuild no longer reports pane_delta helper errors before hitting later blockers","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
